title: alsinopsis douglasii
intro: Alsinopsis douglasii, also known as Douglas' Wood Sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the oxalis family, characterized by its edible leaves and flowers. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of oxalic acid and other compounds. The health benefits of Alsinopsis douglasii include its potential to help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce the risk of certain cancers, and support the immune system. Alsinopsis douglasii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used as a food ingredient in some parts of the world.
